generated_comment: Bhai, aajkal ke chhore baat-baat mein maa-behen wali gaali muh se nikaal dete hain, isme itna sochne ki kya baat hai. Gaali to gusse ki boli hai, seedha matlab thodi hota hai. Aur jo aadmi zara si baat pe pighal jaaye, usko mard thodi kahenge.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it normalizes female-relative-based abuse as harmless 'just expression' and also uses masculinity/femininity coding to shame emotional men. It encodes the frame's core defense: gaalis are dismissed as meaningless even though they rely on degrading women.
original_frame: Other Man's Ownership Over Woman's Own Consent.
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 48. A Jat farmer in a village in Haryana. Your 20 year old daughter Sunita came back from the nearby town where she goes to college and you found out from a neighbour that she has been seen talking to a boy from a lower caste near the college gate. You have not spoken to Sunita yet. You are sitting with your brother and two male cousins in the courtyard in the evening. You are telling them what you heard and what you think needs to be done before this goes further.
